32.reason: Condition should be 'front < rear' to prevent infinite loop when queue is empty.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  The condition should be `front < rear` instead of `front <= rear` to avoid accessing an empty queue when front equals rear.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Condition should be 'front < rear' to prevent infinite loop when queue is empty.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Loop condition should be front < rear; using <= causes an extra iteration with invalid queue data when the queue is empty.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Condition should be front < rear to prevent processing an extra invalid element when the queue is empty.

69.reason: [Using \n in scanf format string can cause input reading issues, especially if the next input doesn't start on a new line. It may lead to unexpected blocking or skipping inputs.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  The newline in the format string may cause input issues; it's safer to use `scanf("%d %d", &nv, &ne);` without the trailing newline.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  The newline in the format string may cause input issues; it's safer to use `scanf("%d %d", &nv, &ne);` without the newline.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  Trailing \n in scanf format string causes it to wait for non-whitespace input, potentially hanging the program or causing parsing issues.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  The \n in scanf format string can cause input buffering issues and should be removed.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Using \n in scanf format string can cause input reading issues, especially if the next input doesn't start on a new line. It may lead to unexpected blocking or skipping inputs.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The '\n' in scanf format string may consume unexpected whitespace and cause input reading issues, especially if the next input is on the same line or lacks trailing newline.]
